From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1997 single by Jay-Z featuring Mecca

| "Feelin' It" | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") by [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") featuring Mecca | |
| from the album  *[Reasonable Doubt](/wiki/Reasonable_Doubt_(album) "Reasonable Doubt (album)")* | |
| [B-side](/wiki/A-side_and_B-side "A-side and B-side") | "Friend or Foe" |
| Released | April 15, 1997 |
| Recorded | 1995 |
| [Studio](/wiki/Recording_studio "Recording studio") | [D&D Studios](/wiki/D%26D_Studios "D&D Studios") ([New York City, New York](/wiki/New_York_City,_New_York "New York City, New York")) |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | * [East Coast hip hop](/wiki/East_Coast_hip_hop "East Coast hip hop") * [jazz rap](/wiki/Jazz_rap "Jazz rap") |
| Length | 3:48 |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | * [Roc-A-Fella](/wiki/Roc-A-Fella_Records "Roc-A-Fella Records") * [Priority](/wiki/Priority_Records "Priority Records") |
| [Songwriters](/wiki/Songwriter "Songwriter") | * [Shawn Carter](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") * [David Willis](/wiki/Ski_(producer) "Ski (producer)") |
| [Producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") | [Ski](/wiki/Ski_(producer) "Ski (producer)") |
|  | |
| [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") singles chronology | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | "[I'll Be](/wiki/I%27ll_Be_(Foxy_Brown_song) "I'll Be (Foxy Brown song)")" (1997) | "**Feelin' It**" (1997) | "[Who You Wit](/wiki/Who_You_Wit "Who You Wit")" (1997) | | |
|  | |

"**Feelin' It**" is the fourth and final single from [rapper](/wiki/Rapping "Rapping") [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z")'s debut album *[Reasonable Doubt](/wiki/Reasonable_Doubt_(album) "Reasonable Doubt (album)")*. The song features a chorus sung by Mecca and a beat produced by [Ski](/wiki/Ski_(producer) "Ski (producer)"). The song's beat contains a [sample](/wiki/Sampling_(music) "Sampling (music)") from "Pastures" by [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") musician [Ahmad Jamal](/wiki/Ahmad_Jamal "Ahmad Jamal"). The track was originally meant to be a [Camp Lo](/wiki/Camp_Lo "Camp Lo") song, but producer Ski gave it to Jay at the last minute. He describes the situation in an *[XXL Magazine](/wiki/XXL_(magazine) "XXL (magazine)")* article:

> It was me and Geechi Suede from Camp Lo, it was my hook and everything. Jay heard it and was like, "I want that record. I don't care what you do, I want that record." I didn't want to give it to him, but I had to because I knew he was going to be the man at the time. So I said, "Fuck it, take the record." It really was me and Suede from Camp Lo, the flow and everything, the way he was flowing on it. That's the way we was flowing on it. So he just took the whole thing. But you know, he killed it in his own way.[[1]](#cite_note-xxl-1)

The chorus of the song was mocked by rapper [50 Cent](/wiki/50_Cent "50 Cent") in his song "Be a Gentleman", from his 2002 [mixtape](/wiki/Mixtape "Mixtape"), *[Guess Who's Back?](/wiki/Guess_Who%27s_Back%3F "Guess Who's Back?")*, and is a diss song against Jay-Z.
